pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 11-12 and 26 confirm path construction; setup_task.sh line 42 confirms TIFF conversion target
setup_task.sh line 48 creates scale_info.txt stating 'Pixel size: 5.0 um/pixel'; also confirmed in task.json description
setup_task.sh line 12 defines RESULTS_DIR=/home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/skeleton; export_result.sh line 6 confirms

Downloaded leaf.jpg from https://imagej.net/images/leaf.jpg (507x446 RGB image), converted to grayscale, applied Otsu thresholding (threshold=182), removed small objects, skeletonized, and analyzed connected components using scipy.ndimage.label and skimage.morphology
Same preprocessing as above: junctions detected by counting skeleton pixels with 3+ neighbors (8-connected). Python analysis of actual leaf skeleton image confirmed 558 junction points.

Measured maximum branch length in skeleton: labeled connected components, counted pixels per component, multiplied by pixel_scale (5.0 um/pixel). Maximum branch length = 260 pixels × 5.0 um/pixel = 1300.0 um.

privileged_info_summary: The leaf_vein_skeleton_analysis task uses real data from the public ImageJ website (https://imagej.net/images/leaf.jpg). The input is a 507×446 pixel RGB leaf image converted to grayscale. The task specifies a spatial calibration of 5.0 micrometers per pixel, which is documented in setup_task.sh and confirmed in the scale_info.txt file. Skeleton analysis of the actual image reveals 2382 detected branches, 558 junctions, and a maximum branch length of 1300 micrometers, all significantly exceeding the minimum thresholds (10 branches, 5 junctions, 100 micrometers). The expected output directory is ~/Fiji_Data/results/skeleton/ and the input is stored at ~/Fiji_Data/raw/leaf/leaf_grayscale.tif.
